value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Known general pattern (hot, humid, tropical, no pronounced dry/wet extreme) but not verified with dated figures

fact_label: Comfortable monthly living range (rent + food + entertainment)
notes: A single person can live comfortably excluding rent on roughly RM 2,100-3,500 (~$470-783)/month

fact_label: Concentrated digital-nomad district (like Goa's Anjuna-Assagao or Da Nang's An Thuong)
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: A real, named open question distinguishing Penang's retiree/expat texture from the other two candidates' concentrated nomad-corridor pattern

value_raw: In-city — Gleneagles Penang (380 beds, JCI+MSQH), Island Hospital, Penang Adventist
notes: Formally designated a medical-tourism hub by the Malaysia Healthcare Travel Council (MHTC); 2026 is Malaysia's formally designated "Year of Medical Tourism" (MYMT 2026) — a genuinely stronger, more independently-confirmed healthcare picture than Goa's or Da Nang's files found this session

value_raw: Long-established retiree/expat destination, especially George Town
notes: No hard headcount found this pass

fact_label: Foreign-buyer minimum purchase price — landed (freehold house)
notes: The sharpest state-level property finding of this pass
A third of the island's landed threshold

fact_label: Global Peace Index-driven local safety read
value_raw: Normal-precautions destination — petty theft, vendor overcharging, traffic, heat/humidity are the likely issues, not violent crime
notes: Penang specifically does not carry a reputation for routine serious attacks on tourists

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: A real gap distinct from Da Nang's and Goa's files, both of which surfaced at least advertised-plan numbers

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Not specifically searched for

fact_label: Medevac line — conditions not treatable locally
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. A real, named gap in Penang-specific medevac-line sourcing

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Same project-wide gap flagged everywhere else this session

23 contributors/371 entries-12mo — a middling confidence tier relative to Da Nang's (68 contributors) and Goa's (15) same-session Numbeo pulls. Reads as the least expensive of this session's three candidates on this specific rent metric, not independently reconciled across currencies/methodology

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Same gap flagged for Goa and Da Nang this session
Among the highest state-level thresholds in Malaysia.

value_raw: Penang General Hospital (Hospital Pulau Pinang) — tertiary referral hospital for northern Malaysia, active Ministry of Health (KKM)-backed expansion underway
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. Confirms system capability extends to the public tier, not just the private/medical-tourism layer already documented — the specific check this backfill pass was asked to run

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: The state-level minimum-price thresholds above are a real regulatory floor, not a full market price survey
Condos/houses specifically RM 3,000-9,000 in Tanjung Bungah; beachfront, popular with retirees and remote workers
The UNESCO heritage core

value_raw: Property mechanism (price-threshold gate) structurally closer to conventional freehold than Vietnam's leasehold/quota system
notes: Not independently tested against a real group scenario this pass
Half the island's threshold — a real, sharp within-state divergence under the same 'Penang' label.

value_raw: Genuinely welcoming, English-speaking community, communication barriers rarely exist
notes: A real structural advantage over Goa's/Da Nang's non-English-first environments; whether this reads differently for a younger, non-retiree remote worker specifically wasn't evaluated

value_raw: Legal for non-Muslims 21+; among the world's highest alcohol excise duties, raised again late 2025; Sharia-law penalties (incl. caning) for Muslims
notes: A genuine budget line, not a cheap everyday indulgence the way it is in Goa

value_raw: Explicitly discouraged — repeated short visits can raise red flags or trigger a ban
notes: A meaningfully stricter posture than Vietnam's currently-tolerated border-run culture, though not backed by Thailand's level of dated enforcement statistics

value_raw: Illegal; mandatory death penalty for trafficking abolished April 2023, but death remains a lawful available sentence; 200g cannabis/50g resin presumed trafficking
notes: Sits between Goa's regime (no death-penalty threshold) and Vietnam's (firm, unmodified death-penalty law) — a real comparative position, not an undifferentiated "illegal"

fact_label: DE Rantau accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: Not yet checked
notes: Built around active remote-employment/freelance income specifically (same shape as Thailand's DTV Workcation category) — not independently confirmed whether passive/investment income qualifies

fact_label: DE Rantau Nomad Pass — income threshold
notes: 12-month pass, renewable once; launched 2022, expanded 2025. The only purpose-built digital-nomad visa found across this session's three new Southeast Asian candidates

value_raw: 13th globally, 2nd in Southeast Asia
notes: Attributed to consistent political stability, low internal-conflict levels, a notable decrease in violent crime rates — trailing only Singapore regionally

value_raw: Not yet checked
notes: No lived account of the state-approval process or price-threshold enforcement found this pass

fact_label: Local private health-insurer new-enrollee age ceiling
value_raw: Commonly 60-70, some plans to 70-75; international policies commonly extend to ~75
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. Directly relevant to the Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) program's own insurance requirement above and to anyone self-insuring outside MM2H — the age-gated compound-gate this backfill pass was specifically dispatched to check and name precisely

fact_label: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) converts to permanent residency
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Whether any route (MM2H, DE Rantau, Employment Pass) converts to genuine Malaysian PR status, and under what timeline, is a real, named open question

fact_label: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) fixed-deposit partial withdrawal
value_raw: Up to 50% withdrawable after meeting approved conditions, incl. completing the qualifying property purchase
notes: A real flexibility — the deposit isn't fully locked for the visa's duration once the property condition is met

fact_label: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) Gold tier — deposit/income requirement
value_raw: RM 500,000 (~$107,000) fixed deposit + RM 10,000/month (~$2,140) income + RM 500,000+ liquid assets
notes: Same compulsory-property-purchase add-on as Silver. SEZ and Platinum tier thresholds not confirmed with precise figures this pass

fact_label: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) medical insurance requirement — age gate
value_raw: Compulsory Malaysia-approved medical insurance for applicants under 60; case-by-case assessment, exemption possible, for 60+
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. A third, separate compulsory gate on top of the Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) program's already-documented deposit-plus-income-plus-property structure, not folded into it. An Immigration Department exemption path exists for 60+ applicants who genuinely can't secure coverage, but the approval process itself (rate, timeline, documentation) is untested this pass

fact_label: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) Silver tier — deposit/income requirement
value_raw: RM 150,000 (~$32,000) fixed deposit + RM 5,000/month (~$1,070) offshore income + RM 150,000+ liquid assets
notes: Age 50+ gate. As of the 2026 restructuring, ALSO requires a mandatory qualifying property purchase (see the MY-penang-scoped state-threshold rows below) — "deposit-gated" understates the real structure: a dual gate, not deposit alone

value_raw: Banned: Akita, American Bulldog, Dogo Argentino, Fila Brasileiro, Japanese Tosa, Neapolitan Mastiff, among others
notes: No equivalent breed-restriction category exists for cats — a real dog-specific rule with no feline analogue, exactly the divergence this project's pet-import convention exists to catch

fact_label: Pet import (dog and cat) — quarantine requirement
value_raw: Compulsory minimum 7-day quarantine, extendable up to 6 months case-by-case
notes: The strictest quarantine baseline of this session's three Southeast Asian candidates — unlike India's/Vietnam's "usually not required with complete paperwork" framing, Malaysia's default is mandatory quarantine regardless of documentation quality

value_raw: No quarantine for pets from UK/Eire/N. Ireland/NZ/Japan/Sweden/Brunei/Australia/Singapore, born/resident there 6+ months
notes: A meaningful, if narrow, carve-out with no equivalent in India's or Vietnam's rules as researched

fact_label: Private healthcare non-citizen Sales and Service Tax (SST) surcharge
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. Private care only — government and university hospital care stays exempt from the Sales and Service Tax (SST) for everyone, citizen or not. In force since July 2025, fully enforced 2026

value_raw: Both freehold and leasehold purchasable, gated by a state-set minimum price threshold, not a blanket restriction
notes: More permissive than India's near-total bar; a distinct mechanism from Thailand's condo quota or Vietnam's leasehold structure — a price floor, not a unit-type restriction. Malay Reserved Land is a separate restricted category

fact_label: Public-hospital outpatient consultation fee — citizen vs. non-citizen
value_raw: RM40 (foreigner) vs. RM1 (citizen)
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. Foreigners are not barred from Malaysia's public hospitals — a real access route distinct from the private/medical-tourism layer — but pay the full unsubsidized non-citizen rate, raised again in 2026 as part of a stated cost-recovery shift

fact_label: Public-hospital specialist outpatient consultation fee — citizen vs. non-citizen
value_raw: RM100-120 (foreigner) vs. RM3 (citizen)
notes: 2026-07-11 healthcare-criterion backfill. Same non-citizen-vs-citizen gap as the outpatient row above, specialist tier

value_raw: open-freehold
notes: Flagged atypical instance: foreigners can buy nationwide, no citizenship/geography restriction — but every transaction is gated by a state-set minimum price plus state-government approval, which the open-freehold token doesn't fully capture.

fact_label: State government approval required for foreign-buyer transactions
value_raw: Yes
notes: Required on top of clearing the price threshold

value_raw: Yes
notes: Malay Reserved Land is a separate, wholly excluded category.

value_raw: Yes
notes: Same price-threshold gate applies as freehold; term length not documented this pass.

value_raw: legitimate
legitimate
90 days for US/UK/EU/Japan/Australia/Canada; varies (90/30/14 days) by nationality
notes: No visa fee/application for the named nationalities — a more generous baseline than India's or Vietnam's paid e-visas. Chinese nationals face a new Feb 2026 cap (30 days/entry, 90 within any 180-day period); Indian nationals get 30 days, extended through Dec 31, 2026

value_raw: No
notes: Immigration Department does not permit extensions of either the tourist e-visa or Social Visit Pass — must leave and re-enter for a fresh stamp

value_raw: PM Anwar Ibrahim's multi-party coalition — one source calls it an 'unstable coalition,' another credits it with real stability
notes: Next election not due until 2027; a genuine, sourced tension named rather than resolved either direction; no concrete dated flashpoint found this pass

fact_label: Coalition fragility (2026) — snap-election talk and two state-election tests
value_raw: Anwar raised the prospect of a snap general election in May 2026 amid coalition-tension reporting; Johor (July 11, 2026) and Negeri Sembilan (August 1, 2026) state elections held in quick succession as real, near-term tests of his Pakatan Harapan coalition's standing
notes: Bloomberg ("Malaysia's Coalition Tensions Deepen as Anwar Floats Snap Poll", 2026-05-17), East Asia Forum ("Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are double trouble for Anwar", 2026-06-29; "Malaysia enters election mode in 2026", 2026-02-08), Fair Observer, voiceofemirates.com, all accessed 2026-07. Sharpens the already-flagged "unstable coalition" tension above with real dated events rather than leaving it as an unresolved abstract characterization.

fact_label: Foreign-investment inflows (2025-2026) — a real counterweight to the coalition-fragility finding
value_raw: Johor state alone secured a record RM110 billion (~$25.7B) in investment in 2025; Anwar's China visits in his first two years reportedly yielded ~RM190 billion in deals and investment commitments
notes: Taipei Times ("Malaysia's comeback is built on stability, not spectacle", 2026-06-17), East Asia Forum, accessed 2026-07. Reads the same two-track shape already found elsewhere in this dataset (Thailand's BOI/nominee split, Mexico's FDI-resilience-despite-USMCA-uncertainty) — real foreign capital continuing to flow in even as the coalition's own political durability is genuinely contested.
